mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 200) triggers central bank tightening, increasing bond yields and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 growth and UNRATE decline explains less than 10% of SPX variance in 60d forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates supply-side shocks, causing equity market sell-offs over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 105 and CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) reflects robust labor market conditions, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no significant correlation with PAYEMS changes (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST > 2200) indicate strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant outperformance when HOUST exceeds 2200.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) and inflation (CPIAUCSL > 200) signal a monetary tightening cycle, reducing equity risk appetite and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show positive correlation with the joint FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL tightening signal (Rank IC > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS > 360000) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS/UNRATE signal explains less than 15% of SPX 5d return variance.
